---
title: LINCS
alias: &ALIAS lincs
href: https://lincsproject.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Library of Integrated Network-Based Cellular Signatures (LINCS) program generates perturbation-response signatures in human cells to build a comprehensive map of how human cells respond to chemical and genetic perturbations.

## Key Features

- Generated L1000 transcriptomic signatures for over 35,000 compounds and genetic perturbations
- Covers multiple human cell lines with dose-response and time-course perturbation profiles
- Provides the Connectivity Map (CMap) for drug repurposing via transcriptional signature matching
- Supports analysis via iLINCS portal and programmatic API access
- Integrates with ENCODE, GEO, and other functional genomics resources

## Use Cases in Research

- Identifying drug candidates with transcriptional signatures that reverse infection-induced gene expression
- Repurposing existing compounds as antimicrobial or anti-inflammatory agents via CMap analysis
- Characterizing cellular responses to antibiotic treatment at the transcriptional level
- Building predictive models of drug mechanism of action from perturbation signature data
- Generating hypotheses for synergistic drug combinations to overcome AMR

## Getting Started

Visit [LINCS](https://lincsproject.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
